definition: To successfully reach a goal or accomplish something through effort
exampleSentence: Many students study abroad hoping to achieve a better understanding of different cultures.

definition: Large or important enough to be noticed or have an effect
exampleSentence: There has been a significant increase in the number of people working from home since 2020.

definition: The natural world, including the air, water, and land where living things exist
exampleSentence: Governments around the world are introducing new policies to protect the environment.

definition: A result or effect of an action or situation, often negative
exampleSentence: The consequence of deforestation is the loss of biodiversity in many regions.

definition: To take part or become involved in an activity or event
exampleSentence: Students are encouraged to participate in extracurricular activities to develop new skills.

definition: Required or essential for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: It is necessary for companies to adapt to changing market conditions in order to survive.

definition: A time or set of circumstances that makes something possible
exampleSentence: Studying abroad provides an excellent opportunity to learn about different cultures.

definition: An advantage or positive result gained from something
exampleSentence: One benefit of regular exercise is that it improves both physical and mental health.

definition: To become greater in size, amount, or degree
exampleSentence: The number of tourists visiting the city has increased dramatically over the past decade.

definition: Systematic investigation to establish facts or reach new conclusions
exampleSentence: According to recent research, regular physical activity can reduce the risk of heart disease.

definition: A group of people living in the same place or sharing common interests
exampleSentence: The local community came together to organize a fundraising event for the new library.

definition: Having the duty to deal with something or having control over someone
exampleSentence: Parents are responsible for teaching their children basic manners and values.

definition: Able to be used, obtained, or reached
exampleSentence: A wide range of online courses is now available to students around the world.

definition: To help someone or something by giving approval, encouragement, or assistance
exampleSentence: Teachers should support students in developing their critical thinking skills.

definition: Relating to trade, industry, and the management of money
exampleSentence: The government introduced new policies to promote economic growth and create jobs.

definition: To influence or cause a change in something or someone
exampleSentence: Climate change is beginning to affect agricultural production in many parts of the world.

definition: To make something smaller or less in amount, size, or degree
exampleSentence: Many companies are trying to reduce their carbon footprint by using renewable energy.

definition: To need something or make something necessary
exampleSentence: Most universities require applicants to submit a personal statement along with their grades.

definition: Different or diverse types of something
exampleSentence: The museum offers various exhibitions throughout the year, attracting visitors of all ages.

definition: To start or create something that is intended to last for a long time
exampleSentence: The organization was established in 1995 to help underprivileged children access education.

definition: A way of dealing with a situation or problem
exampleSentence: The government needs to adopt a new approach to solving the housing crisis.

definition: To make something better or become better
exampleSentence: Regular practice is the best way to improve your language skills.

definition: An important topic, problem, or subject for discussion
exampleSentence: Environmental issues such as pollution and climate change are widely discussed in the media.

definition: A single person considered separately from a group
exampleSentence: Each individual has the right to express their own opinion on the matter.

definition: A period of ten years
exampleSentence: Over the past decade, technology has transformed the way we communicate with each other.

definition: To give something, especially money, help, or ideas, to achieve a shared goal
exampleSentence: Volunteering is a great way to contribute to the local community.

definition: To roughly calculate or judge the value, amount, or size of something
exampleSentence: It is difficult to estimate the total cost of the project at this early stage.

definition: Belonging to the present time; happening or being used now
exampleSentence: The current education system needs reform to better prepare students for the job market.

definition: A conception or belief about something
exampleSentence: There is a common notion that multitasking makes people more productive, but research suggests otherwise.

definition: All the people living in a particular area, city, or country
exampleSentence: The population of the city has grown rapidly due to migration from rural areas.

definition: Good, hopeful, or showing improvement
exampleSentence: Regular exercise has a positive impact on both mental and physical well-being.

definition: The possibility of developing or achieving something in the future
exampleSentence: The young athlete has great potential and could become a world champion.

definition: Existing or happening before the present time or before the one just mentioned
exampleSentence: The previous year's results were better than what the company achieved this quarter.

definition: A series of actions or steps taken to achieve a particular end
exampleSentence: The application process for the scholarship can take several weeks to complete.

definition: To give something needed or wanted to someone
exampleSentence: Schools should provide students with the resources they need to succeed academically.

definition: A place, person, or thing from which something comes or is obtained
exampleSentence: Renewable energy sources such as wind and solar power are becoming more affordable.

definition: The way parts are arranged or organized to form a whole
exampleSentence: The essay has a clear structure with an introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion.

definition: Based on customs, beliefs, or practices that have existed for a long time
exampleSentence: Traditional teaching methods are gradually being replaced by more interactive approaches.

definition: To keep something in the same condition or at the same level
exampleSentence: It is important to maintain a healthy lifestyle by eating well and exercising regularly.

definition: One of the elements or causes that contribute to a result
exampleSentence: Stress is considered a major factor in the development of many health problems.

definition: The right or opportunity to use or benefit from something
exampleSentence: The internet provides access to a vast amount of information on any topic.

definition: Suitable or acceptable for a particular situation
exampleSentence: It is important to wear appropriate clothing for a job interview.

definition: The way someone acts or conducts themselves toward others
exampleSentence: Children often model their behavior on what they see from adults around them.

definition: Having the ability or qualities needed to do something
exampleSentence: She is a highly capable manager who can handle difficult situations with ease.

definition: The exchange of information or ideas between people
exampleSentence: Effective communication is essential for building strong relationships in the workplace.

definition: To examine things in order to find similarities and differences
exampleSentence: The essay asks students to compare the education systems in two different countries.

definition: Feeling sure about one's own abilities or about something being true
exampleSentence: After months of practice, she felt confident giving presentations in English.

definition: To eat, drink, or use something up
exampleSentence: People in developed countries tend to consume far more resources than necessary.

definition: Physical harm that impairs the value or function of something
exampleSentence: The storm caused extensive damage to buildings along the coast.

definition: To explain the meaning of a word or concept
exampleSentence: The dictionary defines 'sustainability' as the ability to maintain something over time.

definition: The need or desire for a product, service, or resource
exampleSentence: There is a growing demand for skilled workers in the technology sector.

definition: A division or section within an organization dealing with a specific area
exampleSentence: She works in the marketing department of a large international company.

definition: To plan and create something with a specific purpose or appearance
exampleSentence: Architects must design buildings that are both functional and aesthetically pleasing.

definition: Without being affected by; in spite of
exampleSentence: Despite the heavy rain, the outdoor concert went ahead as planned.

definition: To decide or establish something firmly, often after careful thought
exampleSentence: The outcome of the experiment will determine whether the hypothesis is correct.

definition: To grow or cause something to grow, progress, or become more advanced
exampleSentence: The company plans to develop new software to help small businesses manage their finances.

definition: To find something that was previously unknown or hidden
exampleSentence: Scientists have discovered a new species of frog in the rainforest of South America.

definition: Relating to the home or one's own country
exampleSentence: The government aims to boost domestic tourism rather than encouraging travel abroad.

definition: The process of receiving instruction and acquiring knowledge or skills
exampleSentence: Access to quality education is essential for breaking the cycle of poverty.

definition: A change that results when something is done or happens
exampleSentence: The new policy had a positive effect on student attendance rates across the school.

definition: Working in a well-organized way without wasting time or energy
exampleSentence: Modern appliances are more energy-efficient than those produced a decade ago.

definition: To pay someone to work for a company or organization
exampleSentence: The factory employs over five hundred workers from the surrounding area.

definition: To give support, confidence, or hope to someone
exampleSentence: Parents should encourage their children to read from an early age.

definition: The power to be active or do work, often from natural or human sources
exampleSentence: Solar panels convert sunlight into electrical energy for heating and lighting.

definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent
exampleSentence: The construction of the new bridge required an enormous amount of time and resources.

definition: To make certain that something will happen or be the case
exampleSentence: Schools must ensure that all students have access to appropriate learning materials.

definition: The tools, machines, or items needed for a particular activity
exampleSentence: The laboratory is equipped with state-of-the-art equipment for scientific research.

definition: Absolutely necessary or extremely important
exampleSentence: Clean drinking water is essential for human health and well-being.

definition: To judge or assess the value, quality, or importance of something
exampleSentence: Teachers evaluate students' progress through regular tests and assignments.

definition: Information or facts that help prove whether something is true
exampleSentence: There is strong evidence that regular exercise reduces the risk of chronic diseases.

definition: To look at or study something carefully in order to understand it
exampleSentence: Scientists are examining the effects of plastic pollution on marine life.

definition: To be present or real; to have objective reality
exampleSentence: Many species of plants and animals exist only in specific climatic conditions.

definition: To become or make something larger in size, scope, or amount
exampleSentence: The company plans to expand its operations into Asian markets next year.

definition: Knowledge or skill gained through direct involvement in an activity
exampleSentence: She gained valuable experience while working as an intern at a law firm.

definition: To make something clear or understandable by describing it
exampleSentence: The teacher explained the grammar rules clearly so that everyone could understand.

definition: To send goods or services to another country for sale
exampleSentence: Germany exports a wide range of automobiles to countries all over the world.

definition: A distinctive attribute or aspect of something
exampleSentence: The new smartphone has several innovative features that appeal to young consumers.

definition: The management of money, investments, and other financial resources
exampleSentence: A degree in finance can lead to career opportunities in banking and investment.

definition: Able to change or adapt to different circumstances
exampleSentence: Many companies now offer flexible working hours to help employees balance work and family life.

definition: To concentrate attention or effort on something
exampleSentence: Students should focus on understanding key concepts rather than memorizing facts.

definition: All people born around the same time, typically spanning about 25–30 years
exampleSentence: Younger generations are more concerned about climate change than their grandparents were.

definition: Relating to the whole world; worldwide
exampleSentence: Global temperatures have been rising steadily due to increased greenhouse gas emissions.

definition: An aim or desired result that someone works toward achieving
exampleSentence: Setting clear goals is the first step toward achieving academic success.

definition: The strong effect or influence that something has on a situation
exampleSentence: Social media has a significant impact on how young people perceive themselves.

definition: To put a plan, decision, or system into effect
exampleSentence: The school decided to implement a new curriculum focusing on digital literacy.

definition: To bring goods or services into a country from abroad for sale
exampleSentence: Many countries import oil because they lack sufficient domestic reserves.

definition: Money received regularly for work, investments, or business activities
exampleSentence: People with higher incomes tend to have better access to quality healthcare.

definition: Not relying on others for support or control; free and self-governing
exampleSentence: Learning to be independent is an important part of growing up.

definition: To show, point out, or suggest something as a sign
exampleSentence: The survey results indicate that most people prefer public transport over driving.

definition: The production of goods or services within an economy
exampleSentence: The tourism industry is a major source of revenue for many coastal cities.

definition: To give someone facts or knowledge about something
exampleSentence: The website informs visitors about the museum's opening hours and exhibition schedules.

definition: Physical harm or damage to a person's body

definition: A new idea, method, or product that improves upon existing solutions

definition: An established organization, especially one providing education or public service

definition: A tool or device used for a particular purpose, especially in science or music

definition: The ability to learn, understand, and apply knowledge and skills
exampleSentence: Emotional intelligence is just as important as academic ability in the workplace.

definition: Involving or relating to more than one country
exampleSentence: International students bring diverse perspectives to university campuses.

definition: A careful examination or search to discover facts or truth
exampleSentence: The police launched an investigation into the cause of the fire.

definition: To include or affect someone or something as a necessary part

definition: An individual article or unit in a collection or list

definition: Information and understanding gained through learning or experience

definition: A room or building equipped for scientific experiments and research
exampleSentence: Students conduct experiments in the laboratory to test scientific hypotheses.

definition: Allowed or required by law

definition: A position on a scale of amount, quality, or degree

definition: Probably; very possibly
exampleSentence: Students who practice regularly are more likely to perform well on the exam.

definition: Restricted in size, amount, or scope

definition: A particular place or position

definition: Important, serious, or significant in size or scope

definition: The process of controlling and organizing people or resources
exampleSentence: Good time management is essential for students who want to balance study and social life.

definition: To produce goods on a large scale using machinery
exampleSentence: The company manufactures electronic components for the automotive industry.

definition: To find out the size, amount, or degree of something
exampleSentence: Scientists measure air quality using specialized instruments at monitoring stations.

definition: The main means of mass communication, such as television, newspapers, and the internet
exampleSentence: Social media has changed the way people consume news and share information.

definition: A particular way of doing something, often according to a plan
exampleSentence: The traditional lecture method is being replaced by more interactive teaching approaches.

definition: Relating to the armed forces or soldiers
exampleSentence: Military service used to be compulsory for young men in many countries.

definition: The smallest or lowest amount allowed or possible

definition: Relating to the present time or recent times; up to date
exampleSentence: Modern technology enables students to access educational resources from anywhere in the world.

definition: To make small changes to something in order to improve or adapt it
exampleSentence: The architect modified the original design to include more natural lighting.

definition: To observe and check the progress or quality of something over time

definition: Existing in or produced by nature, not made or caused by humans

definition: Harmful, unpleasant, or not desirable

definition: To discuss something in order to reach an agreement
exampleSentence: The union is negotiating with management for better working conditions and higher wages.

definition: A group of interconnected people, systems, or things
exampleSentence: Building a professional network can help you find better job opportunities.

definition: Conforming to a standard; usual, typical, or expected
exampleSentence: It is normal for students to feel nervous before an important examination.

definition: To get something, especially through effort or request
exampleSentence: It can be difficult to obtain a visa for some countries without a confirmed job offer.

definition: Easily seen, recognized, or understood
exampleSentence: It is obvious that regular practice leads to improvement in any skill.

definition: To happen or take place

definition: To function or work; to control the functioning of a machine or system
exampleSentence: The hospital operates a 24-hour emergency department for urgent cases.

definition: A personal belief or judgment that is not necessarily based on fact
exampleSentence: In my opinion, the government should invest more in public transportation.

definition: To arrange items or activities in a structured or systematic way
exampleSentence: Students learn to organize their thoughts before writing an academic essay.

definition: Existing from the beginning; not a copy or imitation
exampleSentence: The original manuscript of the novel is kept in the national library.

definition: The final result or consequence of an activity or process

definition: Considering everything; in general

definition: Used to single out a specific person, thing, or situation

definition: A regular or repeated arrangement, design, or sequence
exampleSentence: Researchers have identified a pattern in the way diseases spread across populations.

definition: To carry out an action, task, or function

definition: To allow something to happen or someone to do something

definition: To convince someone to do or believe something through reasoning or argument

definition: A fact or event that can be observed, often one that is unusual or of interest

definition: Relating to the body rather than the mind
exampleSentence: Regular physical activity is essential for maintaining good health.

definition: A set of principles or rules adopted by an organization or government

definition: The presence of harmful substances in the environment

definition: To say that something will happen in the future, often based on evidence

definition: To protect something from harm, decay, or change
exampleSentence: National parks were established to preserve natural habitats for future generations.

definition: To stop something from happening or keep someone from doing something

definition: A fundamental truth, rule, or belief that guides behavior or reasoning
exampleSentence: The principle of equality is the foundation of a fair and just society.

definition: Something that is considered more important than other things

definition: An established or official way of doing something
exampleSentence: Applicants must follow the correct procedure when submitting their visa documents.

definition: The financial gain made in a business transaction after costs are deducted

definition: Forward movement toward a goal or improvement
exampleSentence: The student has made remarkable progress in mathematics since the beginning of the term.

definition: To support, encourage, or advertise something publicly
exampleSentence: The campaign aims to promote healthy eating habits among school children.

definition: To put forward an idea, plan, or suggestion for consideration
exampleSentence: The committee proposed a series of changes to the school curriculum.

definition: A public demonstration expressing strong disagreement or objection

definition: Relating to or involving ordinary people in general, not private
exampleSentence: Public transport systems are vital for reducing traffic congestion in cities.

definition: To produce and distribute printed or digital material for public reading
exampleSentence: The scientist published her findings in a respected academic journal.

definition: To buy something using money
exampleSentence: Consumers can now purchase almost any product online and have it delivered to their door.

definition: The standard of something as measured against other similar things

definition: A variety of different things within a particular category

definition: Happening very quickly or in a short period of time

definition: A response to something that has happened or been said

definition: The state of things as they actually exist, not as imagined
exampleSentence: Virtual reality technology is becoming increasingly popular in the gaming industry.

definition: Fair, sensible, or based on good judgment
exampleSentence: The price of the hotel was reasonable considering its excellent location.

definition: To be given, presented with, or experience something
exampleSentence: Students who complete the course will receive a certificate of completion.

definition: To identify someone or something from previous knowledge or experience
exampleSentence: Experts recognize the importance of early childhood education for long-term development.

definition: To suggest that someone or something would be good or suitable
exampleSentence: Doctors recommend eating at least five portions of fruit and vegetables each day.

definition: To return to a normal state after a difficult or challenging experience
exampleSentence: It took several months for the economy to recover after the financial crisis.

definition: To mention or describe something; to direct attention to a source
exampleSentence: The report refers to several studies that support the author's conclusions.

definition: To show or be a sign of something; to think deeply about something
exampleSentence: Test scores often reflect a student's ability to study under pressure.

definition: An area of a country or the world with particular characteristics
exampleSentence: The coastal region attracts millions of tourists every year due to its beautiful beaches.

definition: To control something by rules or laws
exampleSentence: Governments regulate the banking industry to protect consumers from financial fraud.

definition: To show or establish a connection between two or more things

definition: The way in which two or more people or things are connected

definition: Connected to and important for what is being discussed or considered

definition: Able to be trusted or depended on to do what is expected

definition: A feeling of comfort following the removal of pain, anxiety, or distress

definition: To depend on someone or something for support or help
exampleSentence: Many people rely on public transport to commute to work every day.

definition: To stay in the same place, state, or condition
exampleSentence: Despite the challenges, she remained determined to complete her degree.

definition: To take something away from where it was positioned
exampleSentence: The surgeon had to remove the damaged tissue to prevent further infection.

definition: To take the place of something or someone

definition: To act or speak on behalf of someone; to stand for something
exampleSentence: The bar chart represents the sales figures for the past five years.

definition: The opinion or belief people have about someone or something
exampleSentence: The university has an excellent reputation for its research in medical sciences.

definition: An act of asking politely or formally for something
exampleSentence: The student made a formal request to transfer to a different course.

definition: A medicine or treatment for a disease; a solution to a problem

definition: To review and make changes to something in order to improve it
exampleSentence: Students should revise their essays carefully before submitting them for grading.

definition: A person who lives in a particular place or area

definition: To solve a problem or settle a disagreement
exampleSentence: The two countries are working together to resolve the border dispute peacefully.

definition: A supply of something that can be used when needed
exampleSentence: Water is one of the most precious resources on the planet.

definition: To say or do something in reaction to what someone else has said or done
exampleSentence: The government needs to respond quickly to the needs of the population.

definition: To make changes to something in order to improve it
exampleSentence: The government plans to reform the healthcare system to make it more efficient.

definition: To return something to its original condition or to bring it back
exampleSentence: The historic building was carefully restored to its former glory.

definition: To limit or control something within certain boundaries
exampleSentence: The new law restricts the use of plastic bags in supermarkets.

definition: The outcome or consequence of an action, event, or process
exampleSentence: The results of the experiment confirmed the scientist's hypothesis.

definition: To make something known or show something that was previously hidden
exampleSentence: The investigation revealed serious safety problems in the factory.

definition: Income generated from business activities, taxes, or investments
exampleSentence: The company's annual revenue increased by 15 percent compared to the previous year.

definition: A dramatic and far-reaching change in the way something works or is organized
exampleSentence: The digital revolution has transformed the way we access information.

definition: The possibility of danger, loss, or harm
exampleSentence: Smoking significantly increases the risk of developing lung cancer.

definition: Relating to the countryside rather than the city

definition: An object that orbits a planet, especially an artificial device used for communication

definition: A feeling of pleasure or contentment from achieving something

definition: A plan of things to be done and the times when they should be done
exampleSentence: Students should create a study schedule to manage their time effectively.

definition: One of the parts into which something is divided
exampleSentence: The library has a special section dedicated to rare and historic books.

definition: The state of being free from danger, threat, or harm
exampleSentence: Airport security has been significantly strengthened since the new regulations were introduced.

definition: To carefully choose someone or something from a group

definition: Higher in rank, age, or experience

definition: A feeling, understanding, or awareness of something
exampleSentence: The program gives students a sense of responsibility and independence.

definition: Easily affected, influenced, or offended by something
exampleSentence: Some people have sensitive skin that reacts badly to certain chemicals.

definition: Forming a unit by itself; not joined or connected to another

definition: A number of similar events or things occurring one after another
exampleSentence: The university is hosting a series of lectures on climate change this semester.

definition: Very serious or extreme in degree
exampleSentence: The country experienced a severe drought that lasted for three consecutive years.

definition: A place giving protection from bad weather or danger
exampleSentence: The charity provides food and shelter for homeless people in the city.

definition: A situation where there is not enough of something available

definition: A sign, gesture, or device that conveys information or instructions
exampleSentence: The mobile phone signal was weak in the remote mountain area.

definition: Almost the same but not identical

definition: Having the training, knowledge, and experience to do something well
exampleSentence: There is a growing demand for skilled workers in the technology sector.

definition: A way to solve a problem or deal with a difficult situation

definition: To some extent or degree; slightly

definition: The full length or extent of something from end to end

definition: Precise and clearly defined; relating to one particular thing

definition: Firmly fixed and not likely to change, move, or fail

definition: A level of quality or expectation that is considered acceptable

definition: A person's social or professional position relative to others

definition: A careful plan or method for achieving a particular goal

definition: The quality of being physically or mentally strong; a positive attribute

definition: A particular manner or way of doing something
exampleSentence: Each teacher has their own teaching style, which affects how students learn.

definition: An area of study or knowledge; the topic of discussion
exampleSentence: Mathematics has always been my favorite subject at school.

definition: To give or send something for consideration, approval, or judgment
exampleSentence: Students must submit their assignments by the deadline or face a penalty.

definition: A particular type of solid, liquid, or gas matter
exampleSentence: The chemical substance was found to be harmful to aquatic life.

definition: To replace one thing with another similar thing

definition: Enough to meet a need or purpose

definition: To put forward an idea or opinion for consideration
exampleSentence: The data suggests that there is a strong link between exercise and mental health.

definition: Right or appropriate for a particular person, purpose, or situation

definition: The amount of something that is available for use

definition: A study that collects information from a group of people about their opinions or behavior

definition: To continue to live or exist in spite of danger or hardship
exampleSentence: Some species have survived for millions of years by adapting to changing environments.

definition: To have an idea that something is true or likely, especially something bad
exampleSentence: Doctors suspect that the disease may be linked to environmental factors.

definition: To keep something going or maintain it over a period of time
exampleSentence: The economy cannot sustain such high levels of growth without new investment.

definition: A sign, shape, or object that represents something else
exampleSentence: The dove is a universal symbol of peace and hope.

definition: The feeling of understanding and caring about someone else's suffering
exampleSentence: People expressed their sympathy for the victims of the natural disaster.

definition: A goal or objective that one aims to achieve
exampleSentence: The company has set a target to reduce its carbon emissions by 50 percent by 2030.

definition: A particular method of doing an activity, usually involving practical skills
exampleSentence: The course teaches students various techniques for improving their reading speed.

definition: The use of scientific knowledge for practical purposes, especially in industry
exampleSentence: Advances in technology have made it possible to communicate instantly across the globe.

definition: Lasting for only a limited period of time

definition: A pattern of behavior or a likelihood of happening in a particular way

definition: A word or phrase used to describe a thing or concept

definition: A set of ideas intended to explain something, based on evidence
exampleSentence: Darwin's theory of evolution explains how species adapt over time.

definition: For that reason; as a result of something just mentioned
exampleSentence: The experiment failed; therefore, the hypothesis must be reconsidered.

definition: An indication of possible danger, harm, or trouble

definition: The business of providing services for people on vacation

definition: A long-established custom or belief passed through generations

definition: To move someone or something from one place, person, or situation to another

definition: To make a complete change in the form, appearance, or character of something

definition: The system or means of carrying people or goods from one place to another
exampleSentence: Public transport in the city includes buses, trains, and trams.

definition: A general direction in which something is developing or changing

definition: Having the usual characteristics or features of a particular type

definition: Without a paid job but actively looking for work

definition: Being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else

definition: Relating to or characteristic of a city or town

definition: Based on truth or reason; acceptable according to the law or rules

definition: The worth, importance, or usefulness of something

definition: A number of different types of the same thing

definition: A machine used for transporting people or goods, especially on roads

definition: A particular form or variant of something that differs from others

definition: A person who has been harmed, injured, or killed as a result of something

definition: Behavior involving physical force intended to hurt or damage

definition: Able to be seen or noticed

definition: Relating to seeing or sight

definition: Absolutely necessary or essential for survival or success

definition: The amount of space occupied or the quantity of something

definition: Done willingly without payment or compulsion

definition: The health, happiness, and prosperity of a person or group

definition: Existing or happening in many places or among many people

definition: Ready, eager, or prepared to do something

definition: To take something back or remove yourself from a situation

definition: A person who sees an event take place, especially a crime or accident

definition: All the people who work or are available for work in a particular area or industry

definition: Worth the time, effort, or money spent

definition: To produce a result, profit, or crop

definition: An area designated for a particular purpose

definition: Relating to education, learning, and scholarly study

definition: To go somewhere with someone as a companion or to happen at the same time

definition: Correct and exact in every detail
exampleSentence: It is important to provide accurate information when completing your visa application.

definition: To claim that someone has done something wrong or illegal

definition: To adjust to new conditions or change to suit a different purpose

definition: The act of adding something to something else

definition: Sufficient in quality or quantity to meet a need

definition: The process of managing and organizing the affairs of an organization

definition: To accept or acknowledge that something is true, often reluctantly

definition: To take up or start using something new, such as a method or practice

definition: At a high level of development or complexity

definition: A condition or circumstance that puts a person in a favorable position

definition: A public notice or promotion designed to sell products or services

definition: An event, matter, or situation being dealt with

definition: To have enough money or resources to pay for something

definition: A mutual understanding or arrangement reached by two or more parties

definition: The practice of farming and cultivating land for food production
exampleSentence: Agriculture employs a large percentage of the workforce in developing countries.

definition: Happening or calculated once every year

definition: A feeling of worry, nervousness, or unease about something

definition: Clearly visible or understood; obvious

definition: A request for help, support, or a change in a decision

definition: A natural desire to satisfy a need, especially for food
exampleSentence: Regular exercise can help improve your appetite and overall health.

definition: A formal request for something such as a job, course, or permission
exampleSentence: The deadline for university applications is usually in January.

definition: To recognize the value or importance of something or someone
exampleSentence: I really appreciate the support my teachers have given me throughout this course.

definition: Exactly suitable and appropriate for a particular situation
exampleSentence: The professor's choice of examples was particularly apt for illustrating the complex theory.

definition: To officially agree to or accept something as satisfactory

definition: The gases surrounding a planet; also the mood or feeling of a place

definition: To fasten or join one thing to another

definition: To try to do something, especially something difficult

definition: A settled way of thinking or feeling about someone or something

definition: To draw or pull something toward you; to interest or appeal to someone

definition: The power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce rules
exampleSentence: The local authorities are responsible for maintaining public parks and roads.

definition: Operated by machines or computers without direct human control

definition: A state where things are of equal weight, amount, or importance

definition: Something that blocks movement or prevents progress

definition: An acceptance that something is true or exists, often without proof

definition: To hold someone responsible for something wrong or bad

definition: A close connection or relationship between people or groups
exampleSentence: There is a strong bond between the members of the sports team.

definition: The official line separating two countries or regions

definition: Short in duration or length; concise

definition: An estimate of income and spending for a set period of time

definition: A series of organized activities designed to achieve a particular goal

definition: The maximum amount that something can contain or produce

definition: To take control of something or to record something accurately

definition: An occupation or profession undertaken for a significant period of a person's life

definition: A class or division of things with shared characteristics

definition: To mark a special occasion with enjoyable activities

definition: A difficult task or something that tests one's abilities

definition: The mental and moral qualities distinctive to an individual

definition: To ask for a price or fee for goods or services

definition: An organization that gives money, food, or help to people in need

definition: A substance used in or produced by a chemical process
exampleSentence: Farmers use chemicals to protect their crops from pests and diseases.

definition: The conditions or facts that affect a situation

definition: A legally recognized member of a country with rights and duties

definition: The typical weather conditions in a particular region over a long period

definition: To fall down or fail suddenly

definition: A person with whom one works in a professional setting

definition: A country or area controlled by a more powerful country

definition: The result of joining or mixing two or more things together

definition: A state of physical or mental ease and relaxation

definition: The activity of buying and selling goods and services

definition: To promise or dedicate oneself to a particular cause or activity

definition: A person or animal that accompanies or spends time with another

definition: To try to achieve something better than others

definition: To express dissatisfaction or annoyance about something

definition: Consisting of many different and connected parts; not simple

definition: A part or element of a larger whole, especially a machine or system

definition: Including everything necessary; thorough and complete

definition: To focus all attention or mental effort on a particular thing

definition: An abstract idea or general notion

definition: A feeling of worry or interest about something important

definition: A judgment or decision reached after careful thought

definition: The state or quality of something at a particular time

definition: To organize and carry out an activity or process

definition: A formal meeting for discussion, especially on academic or business topics
exampleSentence: She presented her research findings at an international conference in Paris.

definition: A serious disagreement or fight between people, groups, or ideas

definition: To join or link things together physically or conceptually

definition: Being aware of something or deliberate in one's actions
exampleSentence: Many consumers are becoming more conscious of the environmental impact of their purchases.

definition: Permission or agreement to allow something to happen

definition: The protection and careful management of natural resources and the environment
exampleSentence: Wildlife conservation efforts have helped protect endangered species from extinction.

definition: Large in size, amount, or degree

definition: To be composed or made up of something

definition: Happening all the time or staying the same over a period

definition: To build or create something, especially a physical structure

definition: To seek advice or information from someone or something

definition: The state of communicating or meeting with someone

definition: To hold or have something within

definition: The information, ideas, or subject matter contained in something

definition: A formal written agreement between two or more parties

definition: A noticeable difference between two or more things

definition: To give money, goods, or time to a person or organization in need

definition: Suitable, practical, and easy to use or access

definition: Based on traditional or accepted ways of doing things

definition: To make someone feel certain that something is true or good

definition: To work together with others toward a common goal

definition: To deal effectively with something difficult or challenging

definition: Central or most essential part of something

definition: Relating to a large company or group of companies

definition: To match or be similar to something; to exchange letters

definition: Advice or guidance given to someone, especially professionally

definition: Involving imagination or original ideas to create something

definition: A time of intense difficulty, danger, or decisive change

definition: A standard or principle used to judge or evaluate something

definition: The expression of disapproval or judgment of faults

definition: The subjects and content taught in a school or course

definition: A formal discussion on a topic with opposing arguments

definition: Money that is owed to someone or an organization

definition: To become smaller, weaker, or less in quantity or quality

definition: To protect someone or something from attack or harm

definition: A shortfall or lack of something that is needed

definition: To show clearly how something works or how to do something

definition: Relating to teeth or dentistry

definition: A sum of money paid as a guarantee or part payment

definition: To make someone feel sad and without hope

definition: To obtain or come from a particular source

definition: To be worthy of something because of one's actions or qualities

definition: The place to which someone or something is going or being sent

definition: The act of causing severe damage or ruin to something

definition: To discover or notice something that is hidden or not obvious

definition: To become progressively worse in quality or condition

definition: The identification of an illness or problem through examination

definition: The food and drink that a person or animal habitually consumes

definition: The quality of being worthy of respect and honor

definition: A situation where a difficult choice must be made between options

definition: An aspect or feature of a situation; a measurement of size

definition: A certificate awarded for completing a course of study

definition: The practice of training people to follow rules or a code of behavior

definition: A reduction in the usual price of something

definition: Unfair treatment of someone based on their group, identity, or characteristics

definition: A disagreement or argument over something important

definition: Clearly different or separate from others

definition: To recognize the difference between two or more things

definition: To give or spread something out among a group of people or places

definition: Showing a great variety of different types of people or things

definition: A house and its occupants regarded as a unit

definition: Most powerful, important, or influential

definition: A preliminary version of a written document or plan
exampleSentence: The first draft of the essay was full of mistakes that needed correction.

definition: Sudden and striking; relating to drama or theater

definition: The length of time that something continues or lasts

definition: Characterized by constant change, activity, or progress

definition: Freedom from difficulty, effort, or trouble

definition: A particular version of a published text or product
exampleSentence: The second edition of the textbook includes updated information and new chapters.

definition: To remove or get rid of something completely

definition: To come out or appear from a hidden or unknown place

definition: The production and release of gas, heat, or light

definition: A strong feeling derived from one's circumstances, mood, or relationships
exampleSentence: The film evokes strong emotions in the audience through its powerful storytelling.

definition: Special importance, value, or attention given to something
exampleSentence: The school places a strong emphasis on developing critical thinking skills.

definition: To make it possible for someone to do something

definition: To experience or meet something, especially unexpected difficulties or situations

definition: To take part in or become involved in an activity

definition: Very large in size, amount, or scale

definition: Intense and eager enjoyment, interest, or approval

definition: Including every part; whole or complete

definition: The act of coming or going into a place; also a written record in a journal

definition: An event or a group of events occurring as part of a sequence

definition: Equal in value, amount, function, or meaning

definition: A long and distinct period of time with particular characteristics
exampleSentence: We are living in an era of rapid technological advancement and digital transformation.

definition: A mistake or inaccuracy in action, speech, or calculation

definition: The act of founding or creating an institution or system

definition: To evaluate or estimate the nature, quality, or ability of something
exampleSentence: Teachers use various methods to assess their students' progress throughout the term.

definition: In the end, especially after a delay or long process

definition: Clearly seen or understood; obvious

definition: The gradual development of something over time

definition: To describe something as larger, more important, or worse than it really is

definition: A case that does not follow the general rule or pattern

definition: More than is necessary, reasonable, or acceptable
exampleSentence: Excessive use of electronic devices can lead to eye strain and sleep problems.

definition: The act of giving something and receiving something else in return

definition: To deliberately leave someone or something out

definition: To carry out or perform a plan, task, or duty

definition: A public display of works of art, products, or items of interest
exampleSentence: The art exhibition featured paintings by both established and emerging artists.

definition: To become wider or more extensive

definition: The cost of something; money spent on something

definition: A person with a high level of knowledge or skill in a particular field

definition: To use something to the fullest advantage, sometimes unfairly

definition: To travel through or investigate something to learn more about it
exampleSentence: The course encourages students to explore different career paths before making a decision.

definition: To reveal something hidden or to make someone experience something new
exampleSentence: Studying abroad exposes students to different cultures and ways of thinking.

definition: An additional period of time or part added to something

definition: Covering a large area; large in amount or scale

definition: Very great in degree; far beyond what is usual or normal

definition: A building, room, or equipment provided for a particular purpose

definition: Complete trust or confidence in someone or something

definition: A popular style or trend, especially in clothing or behavior
exampleSentence: Fashion trends change rapidly, with new styles emerging every season.

definition: Causing death or ending in failure

definition: Extreme tiredness resulting from mental or physical effort
exampleSentence: Many employees suffer from fatigue due to long working hours and lack of sleep.

definition: Possible and practical to achieve or carry out

definition: Literature or stories about imaginary events and people

definition: Very strong, intense, or aggressive
exampleSentence: There is fierce competition among graduates for jobs at top companies.

definition: Relating to money, banking, or investments
exampleSentence: Many students face financial difficulties while pursuing higher education.

definition: To grow or develop in a healthy or successful way
exampleSentence: Plants flourish when they receive adequate sunlight, water, and nutrients.

definition: A prediction or estimate of future events, especially weather or economic trends
exampleSentence: The weather forecast predicts heavy rain for the entire weekend.

definition: A method or rule for achieving something; a symbolic expression in science

definition: A large amount of money or wealth; also luck or fate

definition: Happening or occurring often

definition: To cause someone to feel annoyed or upset because they cannot achieve something

definition: The purpose or role of something; a specific activity or operation
exampleSentence: The main function of a university is to provide education and conduct research.

definition: Forming a necessary base or core; very important

definition: In addition to what has already been said or mentioned

definition: Truly what it is said to be; authentic and sincere

definition: Happening slowly over a long period of time

definition: To give or allow someone something, often formally or officially
exampleSentence: The university granted her a scholarship to cover her tuition fees.

definition: To promise that something will happen or be the case
exampleSentence: Hard work does not guarantee success, but it certainly improves your chances.

definition: To deal with or manage a situation, problem, or object
exampleSentence: She knows how to handle difficult customers with patience and professionalism.

definition: To a high degree; very much
exampleSentence: The professor is highly respected in the field of environmental science.

definition: Unfriendly, aggressive, or showing opposition
exampleSentence: The working environment became hostile after the management changes.

definition: Exactly the same in every way
exampleSentence: The two houses looked almost identical from the outside.

definition: Lack of knowledge or information about something
exampleSentence: Ignorance of the law is not accepted as a valid excuse in court.

definition: To explain or make something clear by using examples, pictures, or charts
exampleSentence: The teacher used a diagram to illustrate how the water cycle works.

definition: The ability to form new ideas, images, or concepts not present to the senses
exampleSentence: Children use their imagination when playing, creating entire worlds from simple toys.

definition: Happening or done without delay; occurring at once
exampleSentence: The patient required immediate medical attention after the accident.

definition: A person who comes to live permanently in a foreign country
exampleSentence: Immigrants often face challenges when adapting to a new culture and language.

definition: To make someone feel admiration or respect through achievement or quality
exampleSentence: The candidate impressed the interviewers with her knowledge and confidence.

definition: A sudden strong desire or urge to do something
exampleSentence: She bought the dress on impulse without trying it on first.

definition: An event or occurrence, especially one that is unusual or unpleasant
exampleSentence: The police are investigating the incident that occurred outside the school.

definition: The capacity to affect the behavior, development, or thinking of someone
exampleSentence: Social media has a powerful influence on the way young people perceive beauty.

definition: The ability to act independently and make decisions without being told
exampleSentence: The student showed initiative by organizing a study group for her classmates.

definition: To fill someone with the urge or ability to do something creative or positive
exampleSentence: Great teachers inspire their students to develop a lifelong love of learning.

definition: An example or single occurrence of something
exampleSentence: There are many instances where technology has improved healthcare outcomes.

definition: To explain or understand the meaning of something
exampleSentence: It can be difficult to interpret the results of a study without statistical training.

definition: To put money, time, or effort into something for future benefit
exampleSentence: Governments should invest more in renewable energy to combat climate change.
